name: Robotics: Hands-On STEM
overview: Robotics is the most parent-visible STEM program you can run. Kids build something they can hold, name, and bring home.
Lessons cover motors, sensors, gears, and simple programming. The curriculum is kit-agnostic — works with LEGO Spike, WeDo, or comparable kits.
Each module ends with a working robot the kids built themselves. Parents take photos. Photos drive referrals.

modules.title: Module 2: Motion
modules.summary: Motors, gears, going forward and turning.

modules.title: Module 3: Sensors
modules.summary: Touch and distance sensors, reactive behavior.

modules.title: Module 4: Logic
modules.summary: If/then on the robot, simple loops.

modules.title: Module 8: Showcase
modules.summary: Kids design and present their own robot.

steps.activity: Warm-up: what does a sensor do?
Build: attach the touch sensor and bumper
Program: when bumper is pressed, back up and turn
Test on the arena floor, iterate
Group demo — every team shows their bot

teacherIncluded: Assessments based on completed builds, not paper tests
projects: A robot that follows a black line
A bumper robot that avoids obstacles
A themed build of the student's choice (showcase day)
prerequisites: None — this is a beginner course. Recommended ages 4–8.
